methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
C12 Quantum Electronics is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Matthieu and Pierre Desjardins that has raised $19.4M to date. The quantum computing sector encompasses 11 comparable companies: 3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private peers including Quantinuum and Infleqtion.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: stage-progression analysis. Using C12's $19.4M in total raised as a valuation proxy, a standard 2.0x multiplier applied to Series A progression yields an implied valuation of $39M. A peer-median approach was not feasible given the quantum computing comp universe lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers with matching valuation characteristics—only 1 adjacent-stage comparable was identified against a minimum threshold of 3 datapoints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $33M to $45M, with a median point of $39M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity band around the stage-progression output. Key uncertainties include the thinness of early-stage quantum computing comparables and the heterogeneity of quantum technology architectures across the peer set, which limits the confidence one can place in sector-level multiples. Additional clarity on C12's technical differentiation, IP position, and capital efficiency relative to the peer cohort would strengthen conviction in this range.
